fields.name: thinking_reached
fields.label: Thinking reached
fields.value: Several mounting approaches explored. Hanging rails rejected — inflexible, institutional-looking, and poor fit for the angles in this stairwell. Command strips ruled out — fail on these walls, require grease-free surface, come off after about a week. Permanent fixings are converging as the right approach. Brian explored more engineered options: sacrificial backing boards, bolt systems, adjustable bar arrangements. The group kept coming back to the simplest thing: a plug in the wall, a screw, and a cap when not in use.

fields.name: blocking
fields.label: Blocking
fields.value: Specific fixing method not yet agreed — simple plug-and-screw vs bolt system vs sacrificial board. Single mount point vs double. Whether to standardise on one height or two.

fields.name: next_action
fields.label: Next action
fields.value: Resolve simplest implementation that gives adequate flexibility. Favour simplicity over engineering.
